Within donation_review.html, transient UI state such as loading flags, error messages, and the currently displayed donation summary is held in in-memory state only and is not stored in localStorage or other persistent browser storage.

components.functionality: Provides the structural container for the Donation Review page, including the page title, main content region for the summary and actions, and space for global notifications.

On initialization, calls getDonationReviewSummary to retrieve the current donation draft, associated fund, display frequency label, processing fee estimate, and total charge amount. Manages loading and error states and exposes the loaded data to child components.

Displays a read-only summary of the donation and donor details returned by getDonationReviewSummary, including fund name, fund description if provided, frequency label, base donation amount, estimated processing fee amount, total charge amount, donor name, donor email, optional donor phone and organization, donation status, and start date for recurring donations when present.

Shows a concise breakdown of the financial aspects of the donation using the values returned by getDonationReviewSummary: the base donation amount, an indicator of whether cover_processing_fees is enabled, the processing_fee_amount when applicable, and the total_charge_amount. Clarifies to the user what amount will be charged at the next step.

Displays informational, success, or error messages related to loading the donation summary and confirming the donation. Shows the current donation.status when available and surfaces any message returned by confirmDonationReview or load errors from getDonationReviewSummary.

Provides primary and secondary actions for the user: a Confirm button to finalize the review and call confirmDonationReview, and an Edit Donation button that navigates back to donate.html with the current donation.id in the query string to modify the draft.

Handles the scenario where getDonationReviewSummary does not return a usable donation draft. In this case, it informs the user that there is no donation available for review and provides a navigation action to start a new donation on donate.html.

layout.grid_system: Implicit centered content column likely based on a 12-column desktop grid with a max-width content container (~960–1140px) and full-width header. Content is visually centered rather than edge-to-edge.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a single, centered main content column beneath a full-width site header. Within this column, elements stack in a simple top-to-bottom order: page title at the top of the column, followed by an optional status banner, then the core review content (either the donation summary + fee breakdown, or an empty-state panel), and finally the actions bar at the bottom of the column. The data loader temporarily sits in the same central column, visually replacing or overlaying the review content when loading.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a single, centered main content column beneath a full-width site header. Within this column, elements stack in a simple top-to-bottom order: page title at the top of the column, followed by an optional status banner, then the core review content (either the donation summary + fee breakdown, or an empty-state panel), and finally the actions bar at the bottom of the column. The data loader temporarily sits in the same central column, visually replacing or overlaying the review content when loading.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global header is full-width with modest vertical height and comfortable padding. Below it, the page title occupies a generous vertical band with large top and bottom margins, mirroring the prominence seen in the mockup. The donation summary panel acts as the main 'hero' block within the content column, taking the widest and tallest card space. The fee breakdown panel sits directly beneath it, slightly less tall but equal in width. The actions bar occupies a shallow horizontal band at the bottom of the column. White space frames all components, with substantial vertical gaps (32–64px) between the title, status banner, main panels, and actions bar.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content is kept airy and editorial: each block (banner, panels, actions bar) has generous internal padding (16–24px) and is separated from the next block by clear vertical spacing. Text and data within the summary and fee panels are not crowded; instead, ample breathing room and simple typographic hierarchy emphasize clarity. Overall density is low, with a lot of white space surrounding and between components to maintain a calm, dignified feel suitable for a nonprofit arts organization.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup’s strong vertical rhythm. The eye moves from the site header down to the large centered page title, then to any status banner just below, and then settles on the donation summary as the primary focal area. From there, it naturally continues down to the fee breakdown for more detailed numbers, and finally to the actions bar where the donor confirms or goes back. Within the header, the eye moves left-to-right (logo → nav → social icons), but within the main column the flow is strictly top-to-bottom, uninterrupted and easy to scan.

layout.overall_description: The Donation Review page sits on a white background with a full-width header at the very top: the organization's formal logo on the left, understated navigation in the center/left, and small social icons aligned to the right, all within a modestly tall bar with comfortable vertical padding. Below this header, the main content is constrained to a centered column roughly 960–1040px wide, leaving generous white margins on both sides. At the top of this column, a large, centered, serif page title occupies its own band of whitespace, with ample margin above and below, echoing a concert program cover. Directly beneath the title, a slim status banner may appear, spanning the full width of the column without feeling heavy. The main hero-like block is the donation summary panel, a wide, softly framed card that fills most of the column width and sits in the upper-middle of the viewport, containing the key donation details. Immediately below it, a matching but slightly less prominent fee breakdown panel continues the story in a more detailed, numeric way. At the bottom of this stack, separated by a comfortable gap, an actions bar stretches across the column, aligning the primary confirmation button to the right and providing any secondary navigation. When data is missing, a centered empty-state card takes the place of the summary and fee panels, slightly narrower and surrounded by even more white space, to emphasize the absence of configured donation details. Throughout, the layout is quiet, spacious, and vertically sequenced, with every component aligned to the same central column to maintain a formal, editorial feel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page shell defines the full-page structure. At the very top of the viewport, it presents a full-width header bar on a white background. Within this header, the logo is aligned to the left with a comfortable left margin, navigation links are positioned center-left in a single horizontal row, and social icons are aligned to the far right. The header’s vertical padding is modest (roughly 16–24px) so it feels present but not tall. Below the header, the main content area is a centered column that spans roughly 80–90% of the viewport width on large screens and is constrained to a max-width of about 960–1040px, with equal left and right margins. This column starts with a generous vertical gap (about 64px) from the bottom of the header. The page title sits at the top of this column, centered horizontally, with large top and bottom margins (48–64px) to give it a program-cover prominence. All other components (status banner, data loader, summary section, fee breakdown, empty state, actions bar) are laid out in a single vertical stack beneath this title, aligned to the same left and right edges of the main column. The page shell provides additional bottom margin (48–64px) below the final actions bar so the layout ends with a comfortable band of whitespace rather than feeling cut off at the fold.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The page shell scrolls normally with the document; the header remains at the top of the page and scrolls away as the user moves down (no fixed or sticky behavior). The main content column remains centered and responsive, scaling down on smaller viewports while preserving the top-to-bottom order.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader appears within the main content column in the vertical position where the donation summary and fee breakdown panels would normally sit—just below the title and any status banner. When active, it occupies the full width of the main column, centered horizontally, with generous vertical margins above and below (roughly 32–48px from the nearest elements). Its height is sufficient to clearly contain a spinner or skeleton state, roughly 1/4 to 1/3 of the visible content area on a laptop screen, but it does not attempt to fill the entire viewport. The loader content (for example, a circular spinner and a short line of text) is centered both horizontally and vertically within its block, reinforcing the calm, focused feel. Surrounding content is either hidden or visually dimmed while loading, so the loader reads as the current focal element within the column without introducing clutter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The data loader is positioned in normal document flow inside the main column but may be rendered as an overlay over its immediate content region, keeping the header visible above. It remains vertically centered within its own block and scrolls with the page if the user moves up or down while loading.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The donation summary section is the primary content block and visually replaces the hero image from the mockup. It sits within the main content column directly below the status banner (if present) or directly below the page title if no banner is shown. There is a comfortable vertical gap of about 32–48px between the element above and the top edge of this panel. It spans nearly the full width of the main column (around 90–100% of the column width), with 24px of internal horizontal padding on both sides and 24–32px of vertical padding top and bottom, giving it a card-like appearance. The panel’s vertical height is flexible based on content, but on a typical laptop it visually occupies about 1/3 to 1/2 of the central content area—the single largest content block on the page. Its content (fund name, schedule, amount, donor details, etc.) is arranged in an elegantly spaced, multi-row layout, aligned left within the card while still feeling balanced in the center of the page. The background is subtly differentiated from the page (e.g., a thin border or a very light tint) to give it structure while maintaining the minimalist aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The summary section is part of the normal vertical flow of the main column. It does not stick or float; it scrolls naturally with the rest of the content, remaining the prominent block the user lands on immediately after reading the title.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The donation fee breakdown panel sits immediately below the donation summary section, aligned to the same left and right edges of the main content column. There is a clear vertical separation of about 24–32px between the bottom of the summary card and the top of the fee breakdown card, so the two are visually related but distinct. It shares the same width and card style as the summary panel (full column width with 24px internal padding and a subtle border or tint) but typically appears slightly shorter in height, occupying roughly 1/4 to 1/3 of the central content area on a laptop screen. Inside, the numerical breakdown (base amount, fees, total) is laid out in a simple vertical or two-column structure with ample spacing between lines, emphasizing readability over compactness. The entire block retains generous white space around it, ensuring it feels like a calm continuation of the summary rather than dense fine print.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The fee breakdown panel flows naturally beneath the summary section and above the actions bar. It scrolls with the rest of the content and does not have any sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The status banner is positioned within the main content column between the page title and the donation summary section. It spans the full width of the column, with its left and right edges aligned perfectly to the column edges. A modest vertical gap of about 24px separates the bottom of the page title and the top of the banner, and another 24–32px separates the banner from the top of the summary panel. The banner itself is relatively shallow—a horizontal band with approximately 12–16px of vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding—so that it is noticeable without overpowering the title or the summary. Its background uses a soft, tinted color (e.g., pale green for success, gold for caution) with understated text, maintaining the overall refined aesthetic. When present, the eye briefly pauses on this banner while moving downward from the title, then continues naturally to the summary panel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The status banner appears in normal document flow and only when there is a relevant message to display. It scrolls away as the user moves down the page and does not remain sticky or fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The actions bar is the final major block within the main content column and appears below the donation fee breakdown panel (or directly below the summary panel if no fee breakdown is present). There is a generous vertical gap—approximately 32–48px—between the bottom edge of the last content panel and the top of this bar, creating a visual pause before the user reaches the decision point. The bar spans the full width of the content column and is relatively shallow, with around 16–20px of vertical padding. Buttons and controls inside the bar are aligned primarily to the right side of the column: the primary "Confirm & Proceed" button appears at the far right, with any secondary action (such as "Back" or "Edit") placed to its left with comfortable horizontal spacing (16–24px). The background may be subtly differentiated (e.g., a light rule on the top edge or a slight tint) to mark it as a control area while keeping the look understated. Below the actions bar, the page shell leaves an additional 48–64px of whitespace before the bottom of the page to prevent the controls from feeling cramped against the viewport edge.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The actions bar participates in normal vertical flow and is not fixed or sticky. On longer pages, users scroll down to reach it; on shorter pages, it rests comfortably above the bottom margin, framed by white space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty state panel occupies the same general vertical position as the donation summary and fee breakdown when there is no configured donation to review. It appears within the main content column directly beneath the page title and any status banner, with a generous vertical margin of about 32–48px from the element above. Unlike the full-width summary card, this empty state card is slightly narrower to emphasize its special state—roughly 60–70% of the main column width—while remaining centered horizontally within the column. The card has ample internal padding (around 32px on all sides) and a subtly framed style, similar to other panels but with more breathing room. On a typical laptop, it visually occupies about 1/3 of the central content area, with abundant white space on all sides, creating a calm, reflective moment. The empty-state message and any call-to-action button are centered inside the card, creating a clear focal point that draws the user’s attention in the otherwise spacious layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The empty-state panel replaces the summary and fee breakdown in normal document flow when there is no donation data. It scrolls with the rest of the page and maintains its centered alignment and spacious margins at all times.

name: Auditions & Registration
title: Auditions & Registration
description: Search youth choir audition slots by age, location, date, and time of day, then register a child for a specific audition time.

core_features: Initialize audition filters (age range, location, month, time of day) using getAuditionFilterOptions and any URL parameters provided.
Search and list audition slots using searchAuditionSlots based on the selected child age, location, month, time of day, and weekday-only preference.
Allow users to change the displayed month (including navigating to the next month) and refresh the visible audition slots using searchAuditionSlots.
Display a detailed view for a selected audition slot (including location and formatted date/time) using getAuditionSlotDetails.
Open a registration form for a chosen audition slot, prefilled with that slot’s information, and submit the form using registerForAuditionSlot.
Provide validation feedback for required registration fields (child and guardian details) before calling registerForAuditionSlot.
Support deep-linking to preconfigured filters and specific audition slots using URL parameters (age, location_id, year, month, time_of_day, weekday_only, audition_slot_id).
Update the URL parameters when filters, month, or selected slot change so that the page state can be shared or revisited.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1 – Arrive with preselected filters (e.g., from index.html or ensembles.html):
1. User clicks a link on another page that includes URL parameters such as age, location_id, year, month, time_of_day, and weekday_only.
2. Auditions & Registration page reads URL parameters on load.
3. Page calls getAuditionFilterOptions to retrieve available age_options, locations, month_options, and time_of_day_options.
4. Filter controls are initialized: the age filter is preselected based on the age URL parameter (matching the appropriate age_options entry), the location is preselected based on location_id, and the month/time-of-day/weekday-only controls are set using year, month, time_of_day, and weekday_only.
5. Page calls searchAuditionSlots with childAge (derived from the selected age option or directly from age), locationId, year, month, timeOfDay, and weekdayOnly.
6. Returned AuditionSlot items are displayed in the results list.
7. If audition_slot_id is present, the corresponding slot in the list is highlighted and its details panel is automatically expanded via getAuditionSlotDetails.
8. User may adjust any filter (age, location, time of day, weekday-only), triggering an updated call to searchAuditionSlots and updating URL parameters to reflect the new state.

Workflow 2 – Navigate to the next month and view weekday evening slots:
1. User arrives on the page (with or without URL parameters) and sees the current month of audition slots based on the currently-selected filters.
2. User toggles the weekday-only filter to show only weekday slots if desired; this triggers a call to searchAuditionSlots with weekdayOnly set accordingly and updates the URL parameter weekday_only.
3. User selects the evening option from the time-of-day filter. The page updates the timeOfDay argument in searchAuditionSlots and updates the time_of_day URL parameter.
4. User clicks the next-month control in the month navigation. The page calculates the next month and year, updates the visible month selector, updates the year and month URL parameters, and calls searchAuditionSlots with the updated year and month (plus the current childAge, locationId, timeOfDay, and weekdayOnly values).
5. The results list refreshes to display audition slots in the new month that match the weekday/evening criteria.
6. User can navigate forward or backward through months, with each change updating the URL and refreshing the search results via searchAuditionSlots.

Workflow 3 – Select a specific audition slot and complete registration:
1. From the audition slot results list, user clicks a slot row or a dedicated “Select” or “View details” action.
2. The page records the selected auditionSlotId in its internal state and updates the audition_slot_id URL parameter.
3. The page calls getAuditionSlotDetails with auditionSlotId to retrieve the slot’s metadata, including location, remaining_capacity, is_weekday, display_date, and display_time_range.
4. A slot detail panel opens (inline, side panel, or modal) displaying the information from getAuditionSlotDetails.
5. User clicks a “Register for this audition” action within the detail panel.
6. A registration form opens for that auditionSlotId. The form includes fields for child_first_name, child_last_name, child_age, guardian_name, guardian_phone, and guardian_email.
7. User completes the form and submits it.
8. Client-side validation checks that all required fields are present and that child_age is a valid number; if invalid, errors are displayed and no API call is made.
9. On successful validation, the page calls registerForAuditionSlot with auditionSlotId and the entered child/guardian details.
10. If registerForAuditionSlot returns success: true, the page can display a confirmation message using the returned message and may optionally reflect the registration in the UI (for example, by disabling further registrations for that slot in this session or refreshing details via getAuditionSlotDetails to show updated remaining_capacity).
11. If success: false, the page displays the returned message as an error, allowing the user to correct any issues (such as capacity limits) and try again or choose a different slot.
interactions: On page load, the application reads URL parameters (age, location_id, year, month, time_of_day, weekday_only, audition_slot_id), calls getAuditionFilterOptions to populate filter controls, maps URL values onto those controls, and then calls searchAuditionSlots to load the initial set of AuditionSlot records.

When a user changes the age filter, the selected value is mapped to a specific childAge (either directly via a numeric value or derived from the selected age_options range), URL parameter age is updated, and searchAuditionSlots is called again with the new childAge while preserving the current locationId, year, month, timeOfDay, and weekdayOnly values.

When a user selects a location in the location filter, location_id in the URL is updated, and searchAuditionSlots is re-run using the new locationId and the current values for childAge, year, month, timeOfDay, and weekdayOnly.

When a user changes the month via a month picker or previous/next controls, year and month URL parameters are updated, and searchAuditionSlots is called with the new year and month and the existing childAge, locationId, timeOfDay, and weekdayOnly filters.
When a user changes the time-of-day filter, the selected timeOfDay value is written to the time_of_day URL parameter, and searchAuditionSlots is re-executed with the updated timeOfDay filter.
When a user toggles the weekday-only option, the weekday_only URL parameter is updated to reflect the new boolean value, and searchAuditionSlots is called with the updated weekdayOnly flag.

When a user clicks an audition slot item, the corresponding auditionSlotId is stored, audition_slot_id in the URL is updated, getAuditionSlotDetails is called, and the detail panel for that slot is opened or focused.

When a user opens the registration form for a slot, the form is pre-associated with that auditionSlotId; on form submission, the inputs are validated, and registerForAuditionSlot is called. Depending on the success flag in the response, the UI shows either a confirmation state or an error state. The page may optionally re-fetch slot details using getAuditionSlotDetails to reflect updated remaining_capacity.

When the browser back/forward buttons are used, the page watches for URL parameter changes (e.g., different month, filters, or audition_slot_id) and re-applies them by updating the filter controls and calling searchAuditionSlots and/or getAuditionSlotDetails as needed.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page uses URL parameters as the single source of truth for persistent state across navigation and reloads. On initial load, the page reads age, location_id, year, month, time_of_day, weekday_only, and audition_slot_id from the URL, if present. These values are used to configure the initial filter state and, where applicable, the selected audition slot.

Filter State Mapping:
- age: If provided, this numeric value is used to either directly set the childAge filter or to select the corresponding age_options entry from getAuditionFilterOptions whose min_age/max_age include this age. The resulting childAge is passed to searchAuditionSlots.
- location_id: If provided, the location filter is set to the matching Location.id from the locations array returned by getAuditionFilterOptions. This value is passed as locationId to searchAuditionSlots.
- year and month: If provided, these values determine the initial month in the month filter/navigation and are passed to searchAuditionSlots as year and month. If not present, the page can default to any appropriate month based on the month_options returned, but that default should also be reflected in the URL once determined.
- time_of_day: If provided, the time-of-day filter is initialized to the matching option key from time_of_day_options and passed as timeOfDay to searchAuditionSlots.
- weekday_only: If provided, it is parsed as a boolean to set the weekday-only toggle and passed as weekdayOnly to searchAuditionSlots.
- audition_slot_id: If provided, after searchAuditionSlots returns results, the page checks whether the specified auditionSlotId is among the returned items. If so, it is treated as the selected slot, getAuditionSlotDetails is called, and its detail panel is expanded. If it is not present in the initial result set, the page may optionally still call getAuditionSlotDetails to show details even if that slot is outside the current filtered list.

Every time the user updates a filter, navigates to a different month, or selects a different audition slot, the corresponding URL parameters are updated to keep the URL in sync with the current state. For example, changing the month overwrites year and month; toggling weekday-only updates weekday_only; changing time-of-day updates time_of_day; selecting a different slot updates audition_slot_id. The page avoids using localStorage or other client-side persistence so that the URL fully captures the state.

When the URL changes via browser navigation (back/forward) or programmatic updates, the page re-derives filter and selection state from the URL and makes fresh calls to searchAuditionSlots and getAuditionSlotDetails as needed to ensure the displayed data is consistent with the URL-defined state.

components.functionality: On page load, fetches filter options and maps incoming URL parameters to initial filter selections and search criteria.
event_handlers: onPageLoad: call getAuditionFilterOptions, read URL params (age, location_id, year, month, time_of_day, weekday_only, audition_slot_id), derive initial childAge and filter selections, then trigger searchAuditionSlots via the audition-search-results component.
onUrlChange: when URL parameters change due to browser navigation, re-derive filter selections and trigger searchAuditionSlots to keep view consistent.

components.functionality: Provides interactive controls to filter audition slots by age/grade, location, time of day, and weekday-only option. Updates URL parameters and triggers search when filters change.
event_handlers: onAgeChange: update selected age option, compute corresponding childAge (from option min_age/max_age or directly), update age URL parameter, and request new results from audition-search-results using searchAuditionSlots.
onLocationChange: set selected locationId, update location_id URL parameter, and refresh results via searchAuditionSlots.
onTimeOfDayChange: set selected timeOfDay, update time_of_day URL parameter, and refresh results via searchAuditionSlots.
onWeekdayOnlyToggle: set weekdayOnly boolean, update weekday_only URL parameter, and refresh results via searchAuditionSlots.

components.functionality: Displays and controls the current audition month context (with ability to move to next/previous month or select a month), then refetches audition slots based on the chosen month.
event_handlers: onMonthSelect: when user selects a month from available options, set current year/month state, update year and month URL parameters, and trigger searchAuditionSlots via audition-search-results.
onNextMonthClick: compute the next valid year/month based on current selection and month_options, update year and month in URL, and trigger searchAuditionSlots.
onPreviousMonthClick: compute the previous valid year/month, update URL, and trigger searchAuditionSlots.

components.functionality: Executes searchAuditionSlots using the current filter and month state and renders the resulting list of AuditionSlot items. Handles slot selection and forwards the selected slot ID to the detail and registration components.
event_handlers: onSearchRequest: invoked by audition-filter-initializer, audition-filter-bar, or audition-month-navigation with parameters childAge, locationId, year, month, timeOfDay, weekdayOnly to call searchAuditionSlots and store the returned AuditionSlot array.

onSlotClick: when a user clicks a slot item, capture that slot's id as auditionSlotId, update the audition_slot_id URL parameter, and notify the audition-slot-detail-panel to load details via getAuditionSlotDetails.
onInitialHighlight: after loading results, if audition_slot_id URL parameter is present, highlight or scroll to the matching slot in the list.

components.functionality: Shows detailed information for the currently selected audition slot, including location and display-friendly date/time. Provides the entry point to open the registration form.
event_handlers: onSlotSelected: when notified by audition-search-results of a new auditionSlotId (or when audition_slot_id URL parameter changes), call getAuditionSlotDetails with that id and update the panel content.
onOpenRegistrationClick: when user chooses to register from this panel, pass the current auditionSlotId to the audition-registration-form and open that form.

components.functionality: Collects child and guardian information for the selected audition slot and submits an audition registration using registerForAuditionSlot. Handles validation and displays success or error messages.
event_handlers: onOpenForSlot: when opened from audition-slot-detail-panel, associate the form with the given auditionSlotId, optionally pre-filling child_age if a childAge filter is active.
onInputChange: update local form state for child_first_name, child_last_name, child_age, guardian_name, guardian_phone, guardian_email.

onSubmit: validate that required fields are present and child_age is a valid number; if valid, call registerForAuditionSlot with auditionSlotId and the collected fields; handle the response by displaying success or error using the returned success flag and message.

onSuccessfulRegistration: when success is true, optionally clear form fields, lock the form for that slot, and notify audition-slot-detail-panel so it can optionally refresh slot details via getAuditionSlotDetails to reflect updated remaining_capacity.

components.functionality: Centralizes logic for reading and updating URL parameters to reflect filter state, month selection, and the selected audition slot, ensuring that the page state is reproducible via URL alone.
event_handlers: onReadInitialState: at page load, parse age, location_id, year, month, time_of_day, weekday_only, and audition_slot_id from the URL and emit an initial state object used by audition-filter-initializer and other components.
onFilterStateChange: when filters or month selection change (from audition-filter-bar or audition-month-navigation), update the corresponding URL parameters without reloading the page.
onSelectedSlotChange: when a new auditionSlotId is selected in audition-search-results, update audition_slot_id in the URL.

onPopState: when browser navigation changes the URL (back/forward), re-emit the updated state to audition-filter-initializer, audition-search-results, and audition-slot-detail-panel so they can re-run searchAuditionSlots or getAuditionSlotDetails as necessary.

layout.grid_system: Implicit centered content column likely based on a 12-column desktop grid with a max-width content container (~960–1140px) and full-width header. Content is visually centered rather than edge-to-edge.
layout.layout_pattern: All interactive audition components sit within a single, centered main content column beneath the existing site header, page title, and hero image. Within this column, components follow a straightforward top-to-bottom sequence: filter initializer (invisible), visible filter bar, month navigation, search results list, slot detail panel, and finally the registration form. There are no complex sidebars; the layout preserves a simple, editorial, vertical reading order.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All interactive audition components sit within a single, centered main content column beneath the existing site header, page title, and hero image. Within this column, components follow a straightforward top-to-bottom sequence: filter initializer (invisible), visible filter bar, month navigation, search results list, slot detail panel, and finally the registration form. There are no complex sidebars; the layout preserves a simple, editorial, vertical reading order.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content column is centered on the page with a max width of roughly 960–1040px, leaving generous white margins on the left and right. The top of the functional section (filters) begins with ample space below the hero image (about 56–64px). Each major block (filter bar, month navigation, results list, detail panel, registration form) is separated by 32–48px of vertical spacing, mirroring the generous section margins in the mockup. Internally, cards and forms use 16–24px of padding to feel comfortable but not bulky.
layout_strategies.content_density: The page maintains low content density. Each component spans the width of the content column but uses plenty of whitespace inside and around it. Filter controls are arranged in one or two clean rows with breathing room between fields. The search results are a vertical stack of cards with noticeable gaps between them. The detail panel and registration form are not crowded: form fields are grouped with clear spacing and do not try to pack too many controls in a single row.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The user’s eye moves down the page in a deliberate sequence: header and navigation at the very top, then the large 'Auditions & Registration' title and hero image. Immediately beneath that, the filter bar acts as the functional entry point, followed by the month navigation, which subtly directs attention to the current month of auditions. Below that, the search results form the main body of content; when a slot is selected, the detail panel appears directly beneath, naturally leading into the registration form below it. The flow is strictly top-to-bottom, with left-aligned text and controls, anchored within a centered column that keeps everything visually organized.

layout.overall_description: Below the full-width header, prominent page title, and a centered hero image, the auditions experience unfolds within a single, centered content column that feels like a refined magazine layout rather than a web app. The filter bar appears first as a calm, lightly framed band of controls spanning the column, inviting users to narrow auditions by age, location, date, and time of day. Just below, a centered month navigation bar elegantly presents the current month with discreet previous/next arrows. The main body of the page is a vertical stack of audition result cards, each separated by generous white space so that dates, locations, and times are easy to scan. When an audition slot is chosen, a full-width detail panel appears immediately beneath the results, reading like an expanded article section with focused information about that specific slot. At the bottom of this flow, the registration form occupies its own clear block, aligned to the same column, with neatly spaced fields and sections that feel formal and composed. Throughout, everything is grounded in a consistent left-aligned, serif-leaning typographic system within a centered, white canvas that emphasizes clarity, dignity, and ease of reading.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is a non-visual system component that sits logically at the very top of the main auditions section, just inside the centered content column and conceptually above the visible filter bar. It does not render any visible UI, occupy no measurable width or height, and does not affect spacing. From a layout perspective, the visible portion of the page begins with the filter bar; the initializer simply ensures data is ready before the filter bar appears.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: No visual presence; participates only in the page’s data lifecycle. It does not influence layout, scrolling, or positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The audition filter bar is the first visible functional component in the main content area, positioned below the hero image with a generous vertical gap of about 56–64px to create a clear separation. It sits within the centered content column, spanning roughly 90–100% of that column’s width (max ~960–1040px), with equal white margins on the left and right relative to the browser window. The filter bar appears as a light, understated band or card with a subtle border or background tint, and internal padding of about 16–24px on all sides. Filter controls (age, location, date, time of day) are left-aligned within this band, arranged in one or two rows depending on viewport width: on desktop, most fields appear in a single horizontal row with 16–24px of horizontal spacing between them, while labels and inputs have sufficient vertical separation to avoid crowding. The top and bottom edges of the filter bar are clearly defined by white space: about 32–40px below the hero image and about 32–40px above the next component (month navigation), maintaining the calm, editorial feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard, non-sticky block element in normal document flow. It stretches responsively with the main content column and reflows its internal controls as the viewport shrinks, but it does not fix itself to the top of the viewport when scrolling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The month navigation sits directly beneath the filter bar, separated by approximately 24–32px of vertical whitespace to keep the relationship clear but not cramped. It occupies the same centered content column and spans about 60–70% of the column’s width, centered horizontally, so that it feels more like a typographic element than a heavy control bar. The month label (e.g., 'March 2025') is centered within this band, with subtle left and right arrow controls flanking it. The controls share a baseline aligned with the label text, and the entire navigation bar has minimal vertical height—roughly the height of a single line of text plus 8–12px padding above and below—allowing it to feel light and unobtrusive. There is an additional 24–32px of whitespace below the month navigation before the search results begin, preserving the vertical rhythm and reinforcing that this control influences the list that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A standard inline-block/centered control in the normal document flow. It does not stick to the top during scrolling; it scrolls naturally with the page as part of the content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search results form the central body of the auditions page and appear directly below the month navigation, separated by about 24–32px of whitespace. This component spans the full width of the centered content column (again max ~960–1040px), with content left-aligned to maintain a formal, readable structure. Each audition slot or grouping appears as an individual card or row with a light border or subtle background tint, internal padding of 16–24px, and a vertical gap of 16–24px between cards. Dates, locations, and times are arranged so that the most important information (date and location) is left-aligned and easy to scan, with secondary details to the right or on subsequent lines, always avoiding dense clustering. The results list typically occupies the largest vertical portion of the page, potentially extending beyond the initial viewport and encouraging vertical scrolling. There is a generous 32–40px margin below the last result before the detail panel begins, visually separating the list from the more focused information that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A vertically scrolling list within the main document flow. It grows in height with the number of results and pushes subsequent components downward. No internal scrolling containers are used; the browser window handles all scrolling to keep the experience simple and readable.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The audition slot detail panel appears as a full-width block within the same centered content column, positioned directly below the search results list. If a slot is selected, this panel is revealed with a vertical offset of about 32–40px from the bottom of the last result card, making it feel like a distinct but logically connected section. It spans nearly the entire column width and mirrors the card-like treatment: a subtle border or soft background, with 24px of internal padding on all sides. The content is left-aligned with clear typographic hierarchy—slot title and date at the top, followed by specifics such as address, arrival instructions, and required materials, each separated by 8–16px vertical spacing. The panel’s height adapts to the amount of information, but it remains visually calm with generous line spacing. There is a further 32–48px gap below the detail panel before the registration form begins, making the progression from understanding the slot to registering feel deliberate and structured.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears in place within the normal document flow, pushing subsequent content (the registration form) down when visible rather than overlaying or floating above content. It does not use fixed or sticky positioning; it simply expands the page vertically.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The audition registration form occupies the final major block in the main content column, positioned beneath the detail panel with a generous 32–48px vertical gap to define it as a distinct step. It spans the full width of the centered column and uses a card-like structure with subtle edge definition and 24px padding. The form is left-aligned and organized vertically, with sections such as 'Child Information', 'Parent/Guardian Contact', and 'Additional Details' stacked top-to-bottom. Within each section, labels and fields are aligned to a clean vertical grid; most fields occupy the full column width or, at most, are arranged in simple two-column rows with 16–24px horizontal spacing, always maintaining ample whitespace between form rows (12–16px). A primary submit button is placed at the bottom right or left within the same column width, with about 24–32px of whitespace above it to separate it from the last field group, and 48–64px below it before the end of the content area or footer, preserving the open, dignified feel. The overall impression is of a formal paper form translated into a quiet, digital layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A standard block-level form in normal document flow. It grows vertically based on the number of fields and scrolls naturally with the page. No fixed or sticky controls are used; actions occur at the bottom of the form where users naturally end their reading.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is an invisible state-synchronization component that conceptually sits alongside the other data-handling logic, tied to the filter bar, month navigation, and selected result state. It does not render any visible UI and occupies no space on the page. From a layout perspective, it neither creates nor consumes vertical or horizontal room; it simply ensures that the current filter and selection state is reflected in the URL and restored when necessary.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: No visual presence; operates entirely in the background without affecting layout, position, or scroll behavior.

name: Scholarships & Tuition Aid
title: Scholarships & Tuition Aid
description: Page for viewing tuition scholarship options and submitting online scholarship applications for a selected season.

core_features: Load available scholarship types, application seasons, and household income range options using getScholarshipPageOptions.
Interpret incoming URL parameters (season_id, scholarship_type_id) to preselect the scholarship type and season when the page loads.
Allow users to change the selected scholarship type and season via selectors, updating the page state and URL parameters without reloading the page.
Display the currently selected scholarship type’s name and description to clarify what the user is applying for.
Provide a structured scholarship application form bound to the selected scholarship type and season, including singer details, guardian contact information, and financial need information.
Populate the household income range field from dynamic income range options returned by getScholarshipPageOptions.
Submit scholarship applications through submitScholarshipApplication, passing the selected scholarship type, season, and all required form fields.
Show submission states (in progress, success, error) and the response message returned by submitScholarshipApplication.

user_workflows.name: Initial page load with optional preselected season and scholarship type
steps: 1. User navigates to scholarships.html, optionally with URL parameters season_id and/or scholarship_type_id.
2. The page parses the URL query string and extracts season_id and scholarship_type_id if present.
3. The page calls getScholarshipPageOptions and enters a loading state while waiting for the response.
4. When getScholarshipPageOptions returns, the page stores scholarship_types, seasons, income_ranges, and default_season_id in page state.

5. The page determines the selected season: if season_id from the URL matches a returned Season.id, it is used; otherwise default_season_id is used (if available); if neither is available, the first available season is used (if any).

6. The page determines the selected scholarship type: if scholarship_type_id from the URL matches a returned ScholarshipType.id, it is used; otherwise the first available active scholarship type is used (if any).

7. The scholarship type selector and season selector components are initialized with these resolved selections, and the application form is initialized with the same selected scholarshipTypeId and seasonId.
8. If no scholarship types or seasons are available from the API, the page displays an appropriate informational message instead of the application form.

user_workflows.name: User reviews scholarship options and switches scholarship type or season
steps: 1. After load, the user sees selectors for scholarship type and season populated from getScholarshipPageOptions.
2. The user changes the scholarship type selector to a different ScholarshipType.
3. The page updates its selectedScholarshipTypeId state and re-renders the scholarship type detail panel and application form heading to reflect the newly selected type.
4. The URL query parameter scholarship_type_id is updated (added or replaced) to match the new selectedScholarshipTypeId while preserving any existing season_id parameter.
5. If the user changes the season selector, the page updates its selectedSeasonId state and re-binds the application form to use this season for submission.
6. The URL query parameter season_id is updated to match the new selectedSeasonId while preserving any existing scholarship_type_id parameter.
7. The user can scroll or click an 'Apply Now' action to jump to the application form section, which now reflects the current type and season selections.

user_workflows.name: User completes and submits a scholarship application
steps: 1. The user ensures the desired scholarship type and season are selected via the selectors.
2. In the application form, the user enters the singer’s first name, last name, and date of birth.
3. The user indicates whether the singer is a current member (e.g., via a yes/no control bound to isCurrentMember).
4. The user enters the guardian’s name, email, and optionally phone number.
5. The user opens the household income range field and selects an option from the dynamically loaded income_ranges, which sets householdIncomeRangeId in the form state.
6. The user enters a written explanation of financial need into a text area bound to financialNeedExplanation, with optional inline validation (e.g., not empty, minimum length).
7. The user submits the form. The page validates all required fields locally (e.g., presence of singer and guardian details, selected income range, non-empty explanation).

8. If validation passes, the page calls submitScholarshipApplication with the currently selected scholarshipTypeId and seasonId along with all entered form values and enters a submitting state (e.g., disabling the submit button and showing a spinner).

9. If submitScholarshipApplication returns success: true, the page stores the returned ScholarshipApplication object, displays the message from the API in a success state component, and may optionally keep the form visible but locked, or present a simple confirmation state.

10. If submitScholarshipApplication returns success: false or the call fails, the page displays the message (if provided) as an error and leaves the form data intact so the user can correct and resubmit.

user_workflows.name: User shares or revisits a preconfigured scholarship application URL
steps: 1. While selecting a scholarship type and season, the page continuously syncs selectedScholarshipTypeId and selectedSeasonId into the URL parameters scholarship_type_id and season_id.
2. The user copies the page URL from the browser, which now encodes the current scholarship type and season selection.
3. When the user or another visitor later opens this URL, the initial page load workflow runs, reading the encoded scholarship_type_id and season_id parameters.
4. The page reselects the same scholarship type and season (if they are still available from getScholarshipPageOptions) and preconfigures the application form accordingly.
5. The user can then proceed directly to filling out and submitting the application for that preselected context.

interactions.name: Change scholarship type selection
interactions.description: User changes the scholarship type selector to a different scholarship type.
details: Triggered when the user selects a different ScholarshipType option in the scholarship type selector component.
Updates internal state: selectedScholarshipTypeId.
Updates URL: the scholarship_type_id query parameter is set to the new ScholarshipType.id, preserving other parameters.
Refreshes bound components: the scholarship type details panel and application form heading/body reflect the new type.
Does not require additional API calls because all scholarship types are already loaded from getScholarshipPageOptions.

interactions.name: Change season selection
interactions.description: User changes the season selector to apply for a different season.
details: Triggered when the user selects a different Season option in the season selector component.
Updates internal state: selectedSeasonId.
Updates URL: the season_id query parameter is set to the new Season.id, preserving other parameters.
The application form’s bound seasonId is updated so that subsequent submissions use the newly selected season.
No additional data is fetched, because season metadata is already available from getScholarshipPageOptions.

interactions.name: Apply Now scroll/jump
interactions.description: User clicks an 'Apply Now' control to move directly to the application form for the currently selected scholarship type and season.
details: Triggered by clicking a button or link in the filters area.
Does not change application state or URL parameters.
Scrolls or focuses to the application form component on the same page.
Ensures the user sees the form in the context of their current type and season selection.

interactions.name: Form input interactions
interactions.description: User fills out the application form fields.
details: Each field (singer name, date of birth, membership status, guardian details, income range, financial need explanation) updates a corresponding key in the form’s local state on change.
Income range field is a selector whose options are mapped from the income_ranges returned by getScholarshipPageOptions; selecting one sets the householdIncomeRangeId field in the form state.
Validation runs on blur or on submit to check required fields and simple formats (e.g., email structure).
Validation errors are tracked and displayed per-field, blocking submission until resolved.

interactions.name: Submit application
interactions.description: User submits the scholarship application form.
details: Triggered when the user activates the form’s submit button.
Performs client-side validation; if any required field is missing or invalid, submission is blocked and errors are shown.

If validation passes, calls submitScholarshipApplication with arguments built from: selectedScholarshipTypeId, selectedSeasonId, and form state fields for singer and guardian details, income range, and financial need explanation.
Sets a submitting state: disables submit button and optionally shows a loading indicator.
On success, stores the returned ScholarshipApplication object, clears the submitting state, sets a success flag, and displays the returned message in the status component.
On error, clears the submitting state, sets an error flag, and displays the returned message or a generic error message while preserving user input for correction.

page_functionality.state_logic: The page’s primary state is derived from a combination of URL parameters and the result of getScholarshipPageOptions. On initial load, the page parses the URL for season_id and scholarship_type_id. It then calls getScholarshipPageOptions to retrieve scholarship_types, seasons, income_ranges, and default_season_id. Once the data is available, the page resolves selectedSeasonId by preferring a matching season_id from the URL, then default_season_id, then the first available season if necessary. It resolves selectedScholarshipTypeId by preferring a matching scholarship_type_id from the URL, then the first available scholarship type. These resolved IDs become the authoritative state for the selectors and the form bindings. When users change the scholarship type or season via UI controls, the corresponding selected*Id values are updated in memory and synchronized back to the URL query string by updating scholarship_type_id and season_id respectively while preserving any other existing parameters. The application form reads from selectedScholarshipTypeId and selectedSeasonId when preparing the payload for submitScholarshipApplication, ensuring submissions always reflect the latest selections even if the user changed them without reloading the page.

components.functionality: Initializes the Scholarships & Tuition Aid page by reading URL parameters, calling getScholarshipPageOptions, and setting initial selected scholarship type and season.
Displays a selection control for available scholarship types and manages the currently selected scholarshipTypeId.
Displays a selection control for available seasons and manages the currently selected seasonId used for applications.
Shows the name and description of the currently selected scholarship type so users understand what they are applying for.
Provides contextual information and displays the list of household income ranges for reference next to or above the income range selector in the form.
Provides an 'Apply Now' control that scrolls or jumps the user to the scholarship application form section for the currently selected type and season.
Collects all required scholarship application data, including singer and guardian details, household income range, and financial need explanation, and submits it via submitScholarshipApplication.
Displays the status of the scholarship application submission, including success or error messages returned by submitScholarshipApplication.
Keeps the scholarship type and season selections in sync with the URL parameters so links can be shared and reloaded with the same state.
Determines when to show informational messages instead of the application form if no scholarship types, seasons, or income ranges are available from getScholarshipPageOptions.

layout.grid_system: Implicit centered content column likely based on a 12-column desktop grid with a max-width content container (~960–1140px) and full-width header. Content is visually centered rather than edge-to-edge.
layout.layout_pattern: All components sit within a single centered content column beneath the site-wide header. The page opens with a generous title + hero band (handled by the page-init), followed by a compact control area with the scholarship-type and season selectors, then explanatory/info panels, then the primary action anchor and the application form. Status messages appear immediately above the form. Non-visual logic components sit in the flow but do not occupy visible space. Everything is arranged in a simple top-to-bottom stack, occasionally using a two-column subdivision within the main column on larger screens.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All components sit within a single centered content column beneath the site-wide header. The page opens with a generous title + hero band (handled by the page-init), followed by a compact control area with the scholarship-type and season selectors, then explanatory/info panels, then the primary action anchor and the application form. Status messages appear immediately above the form. Non-visual logic components sit in the flow but do not occupy visible space. Everything is arranged in a simple top-to-bottom stack, occasionally using a two-column subdivision within the main column on larger screens.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: A max-width content container (roughly 960–1140px) is horizontally centered with wide white margins on large screens. The page title + hero band occupies a tall, spacious block with large top and bottom margins (around 64px each). The selector row gets a moderate height with clear separation from the title band (around 32–48px). Info panels are given comfortable padding (16–24px) and separated vertically by ~24–32px. The form section is a substantial vertical block, with additional space above and below to keep it from feeling dense. Overall, whitespace is generous between every major component group (approx. 48–64px).
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density stays low throughout: each component has ample whitespace around it, with no cramped clusters of controls. Selectors are clearly separated, labels and fields are spaced with a consistent 8–16px rhythm, and info panels read like short editorial cards. The form uses vertical stacking with clear grouping and padding, avoiding multi-column form density; only the selectors use two columns on larger screens. Text blocks are short and well separated to maintain an airy, dignified feel.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye moves down the page in a controlled vertical flow: first the global header (outside this spec), then the prominent page title and supporting hero band, then down to the selectors where the user chooses scholarship type and season. From there, the eye proceeds to the contextual information panels that explain the options and income guidance, then to the conspicuous 'Apply now' anchor, and finally to the application form itself. When present, the application status banner sits just above the form and momentarily interrupts the flow with a clear message before the user continues downward.

layout.overall_description: Within a centered, white content column beneath the full-width site header, the Scholarships & Tuition Aid page presents itself like a formal program page. At the top of the main content, a tall, spacious band contains the large, serif page title and optional supporting visual, isolated with generous margins above and below so it feels like a cover header. Below this band, the column narrows into a functional but still airy area: scholarship-type and season selectors sit side by side on desktop (stacked on small screens), followed by softly framed informational panels that read like short editorial notes. A clear but understated 'Apply now' anchor signals the transition into the application section, where the form occupies the full width of the content column and stretches vertically with well-spaced fields. Any status message appears as a horizontal banner just above the form, using the same width and alignment. Throughout, components are stacked in a simple vertical sequence, aligned left within the centered column, with broad white margins left and right and generous vertical spacing that keeps the experience calm, legible, and in keeping with an arts nonprofit’s traditional, composed aesthetic.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component establishes the main content region for the page. It creates a centered content column beneath the global header, with a max-width of approximately 960–1140px and equal left/right margins. At the top of this column, it places the page title 'Scholarships & Tuition Aid' in a large serif type, left-aligned within the column but visually centered in the page due to the symmetrical margins. The title sits in its own vertical band, with roughly 64px of whitespace above it (to separate it from the site header) and 48–64px below it. Directly below the title, still within this band, there is room for an optional hero visual or subtle graphic (if used by the design system): it would be centered within the content column, spanning about 80–90% of the column width, with white margins on both sides and 24–32px of space above and below. The bottom edge of this page-init band transitions into the functional area where the selectors begin, leaving about 48px of clear space before the first selector row.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Defines the main centered layout container and top title/hero band; scrolls normally with the page and does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Positioned immediately below the page-init band, this selector appears in the upper left portion of the functional zone. On desktop, it occupies the left half of an internal two-column row within the main content column (approximately 6 of 12 grid columns, or about 48–50% of the column width), with 16–24px of horizontal gutter between it and the season selector on the right. It is rendered as a compact, low-profile control block: a label and control (dropdown or segmented buttons) aligned to the left, with internal padding around 16px and 16–24px of space above and below the block. On smaller screens, this component drops to full width, stacking above the season selector, with about 16–24px vertical spacing between the two. The entire selectors row has roughly 32–40px of whitespace above it (from the title band) and 32px below it (before the info panels).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in a responsive two-column row on larger screens and stacks to a single column on small screens; otherwise follows normal document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Aligned horizontally with the scholarship-type selector, the season selector sits to the right of it within the same row in the main content column. On desktop, it occupies the right half of the row (roughly 6 of 12 grid columns) with a 16–24px gutter separating it from the scholarship-type selector. It mirrors the visual treatment: label and control left-aligned, internal padding around 16px, and similar vertical height so the two selectors visually read as a balanced pair. The top and bottom spacing within the row matches that of the scholarship-type selector, keeping their combined block about 80–120px tall. On smaller screens, this selector becomes a full-width block directly beneath the scholarship-type selector, separated by about 16–24px of vertical space. The bottom of this component is about 32px above the first info panel below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Shares a responsive row with the scholarship-type selector; switches between two-column and stacked layout based on viewport width; otherwise standard flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This info panel is placed directly below the selectors row, occupying the full width of the main content column (minus the standard left/right padding inside the container). There is approximately 32px of vertical space between the bottom of the selectors and the top of this panel, giving it clear separation. The panel itself reads like a lightly framed editorial card: subtle border or background, 16–24px internal padding on all sides, and text left-aligned with a clear hierarchy (heading, then short descriptive paragraphs or bullet points). It spans the central column width, leaving white margins to the browser edges. Below it, there is an additional 24–32px gap before the next info panel, maintaining the calm, low-density rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sits in the standard vertical stack within the main column, expanding vertically as content requires; no sticky or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This panel appears directly below the scholarship-type details panel, aligned to the same left and right edges within the main content column and spanning the same full width. It is separated from the panel above by about 24–32px of whitespace, so the two are clearly distinct but visually related. The income guidance panel may use a slightly differentiated background tone or rule to subtly distinguish it, while still keeping a restrained nonprofit aesthetic. Internal padding is again around 16–24px, with content arranged in short, readable blocks (e.g., brief intro text followed by a simple list or range table). The bottom of this panel sits approximately 40–48px above the 'Apply now' anchor that follows, establishing a clear conceptual transition from explanation to action.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block in the vertical flow; grows vertically to fit content and does not have special positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The 'Apply now' anchor is positioned just below the income-range guidance panel as a concise, textual call-to-action that bridges into the application form. It is aligned with the left edge of the content column, keeping with the editorial layout, and appears with about 40–48px of vertical spacing from the panel above, so it feels like the start of a new section. The anchor itself may be styled as an understated button-like link or bold text link, but it does not span the full width; instead, it is a line-length element occupying only as much horizontal space as the text requires, leaving white space to its right. There is around 16–24px of space below this anchor before the top of the form so that the anchor and form are visually associated but not cramped together.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows inline/within a standard block, participating in normal vertical layout; may scroll the page to the form when activated, but its physical placement is static.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The application form occupies a substantial block directly below the 'Apply now' anchor, spanning the entire width of the main content column. It is separated from the anchor by roughly 16–24px so the relationship is clear yet not cramped. The form is laid out as a vertical sequence of fields and sections, keeping to a single-column layout to preserve readability and the low-density feel: labels left-aligned, fields filling about 100% of the column width (minus 16–24px internal padding if the form is visually boxed), with 8–16px gaps between fields and 24–32px gaps between logical field groups. If the form is within a visual container, that container has 16–24px of internal padding and a subtle border or background to define its edges. The entire form block has generous space above and below relative to the rest of the page: about 48–64px above (counting from the start of its first field to the elements above) and about 64px of whitespace below before any footer content, reinforcing its importance and keeping the section from feeling heavy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard vertically scrolling form within the main column; no sticky sidebars or floating labels implied beyond normal field behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When present, the application status message appears as a horizontal banner directly above the top edge of the scholarship-application-form, sharing the same left and right alignment within the main content column. It spans the full width of the column and sits approximately 16–24px below the 'Apply now' anchor, effectively inserting itself between the anchor and the form. Internally, it has 12–16px of vertical padding and 16–24px of horizontal padding, with text left-aligned and possibly a subtle icon on the left. The banner is visually noticeable—through color or rule—yet still restrained to match the nonprofit aesthetic. There is 16–24px of spacing between the bottom of this banner and the top of the form container, so the banner is clearly associated with the form but does not touch it directly.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered block in normal flow; it pushes the form downward when shown but otherwise occupies no space.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This logic component does not present any visible UI. Conceptually, it is tied to the selectors and the form, ensuring the currently selected scholarship type, season, and possibly form state are reflected in the URL. In the layout, it can be thought of as existing alongside these interactive components but with zero height and width, exerting no visual presence and not affecting spacing between visible elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; occupies no display space and does not influence physical layout. It operates behind the scenes, reacting to and updating state without altering component positions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component governs what appears when there are no applicable scholarships or when required selections have not yet been made. Visually, when it triggers an empty state, it may replace or overlay parts of the details panel or form area with a succinct message block centered within the main content column. That block would resemble a small, editorial notice: left-aligned text in a subtle container with 16–24px padding and ample whitespace (32–48px) above and below. When no empty state is active, the handler itself has no visible footprint and does not affect spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally shows or hides a standard-width message block within the normal flow, typically occupying the same vertical region as the details or form area it is substituting; otherwise non-visual and non-spatial.

name: Events & Concerts
title: Events & Concerts
description: Page for browsing upcoming events, filtering by month/year and event type, viewing detailed event information, and entering the ticket selection flow for a chosen event.

core_features: Initialize event filters by calling getEventFilterOptions and reading optional year, month, and event_type URL parameters.
List upcoming events by calling listEvents with the currently selected year, month, and event type filters.
Allow users to change filters (month/year and event type), which updates the URL parameters and refreshes the event list.
Enable users to open an event detail view that loads full event information, location, and price tier overview via getEventDetails.
Support direct deep links to a specific event detail using the event_id URL parameter, with the detail view opened automatically on page load.
Provide a prominent 'Buy Tickets' or 'Select Seats' button in the event detail view that navigates to tickets.html with the selected event_id as a URL parameter.
Keep page state (filters and selected event) synchronized with URL parameters so that back/forward navigation and shared links reconstruct the same view.
user_workflows: Browsing upcoming events: user visits events.html with no parameters; page calls getEventFilterOptions to load available month_options and event_types; page selects a default month/year option and no event_type filter (or a default if defined by options); page calls listEvents(year, month, eventType) to display events; user scrolls through the list and can open details for any event.

Filtering by month/year and event type: user changes the month/year option in the filter component; filter component notifies the page controller; controller updates the year and month URL parameters, clears any event_id parameter, and calls listEvents with the new year and month; if the user also selects an event_type, the controller updates the event_type URL parameter and re-calls listEvents with the new eventType; the event list updates to reflect the selected filters.

Opening event details from the list: user clicks an event in the event list; the list component emits a selection event with the Event.id; page controller updates the URL to include event_id for the selected event (preserving current year, month, and event_type); controller calls getEventDetails(eventId); when data is returned, the event detail panel shows event information, location, and price tiers; user can close the detail panel, which removes event_id from the URL.

Deep linking to an event detail: user lands on a URL like events.html?event_id=<some-id> (with or without year, month, or event_type); page controller reads event_id from the URL and calls getEventFilterOptions; if year and month are missing, controller calls getEventDetails(event_id) to obtain the event start_datetime and derive its year and month, then selects a matching month_option if available and updates year/month URL parameters; controller calls listEvents with the resolved year, month, and optional event_type; controller ensures the detail panel for event_id is opened using the already-fetched details.

Entering ticket selection from an event: within the event detail view the user clicks 'Buy Tickets' or 'Select Seats'; the detail component triggers navigation to tickets.html with event_id set to the currently selected Event.id; tickets.html then uses this event_id to initialize its own flows (outside the scope of this page).
interactions: On page load, read URL parameters year, month, event_type, and event_id; call getEventFilterOptions and then determine initial filter and selection state from the combination of URL parameters and returned filter options.

When the user selects a different month/year option, update the internal filter state, write year and month to the URL (using pushState or an equivalent mechanism), remove any existing event_id parameter, and call listEvents with the new year and month.
When the user selects or clears an event type filter, update internal state, update the event_type parameter in the URL (or remove it when cleared), and call listEvents with the new eventType value.
When the user clicks on an event in the event list, capture the Event.id, update the event_id URL parameter, and call getEventDetails with that eventId to populate the event detail panel.
When the user closes the event detail panel, remove event_id from the URL and hide the detail panel without altering the current filter parameters.

When the browser back or forward button is used, respond to URL changes by re-reading year, month, event_type, and event_id, then re-issuing listEvents and getEventDetails (if needed) so the view matches the URL state.
When the user clicks the 'Buy Tickets' or 'Select Seats' button in the detail panel, navigate to tickets.html?event_id=<selectedEventId> without altering the current page data.
page_functionality.state_logic: The URL query parameters year, month, event_type, and event_id are the canonical representation of page state. On initialization, the page controller reads these parameters and calls getEventFilterOptions. It then determines the active filters: if year and month are present and match a returned month_options entry, they are used; otherwise, if event_id is present but year/month are missing, getEventDetails(event_id) is called to derive the event's year and month from its start_datetime and, if this combination exists in month_options, those values are set and written into the URL; if neither condition is met, a default month_option is chosen (implementation-defined) and its year and month are written into the URL. The event_type URL parameter, if present and matching one of the event_types returned from getEventFilterOptions, becomes the active event type filter; otherwise, no event type filter is applied and the event_type parameter is cleared. With the resolved year, month, and eventType, the controller calls listEvents(year, month, eventType) to populate the event list. If event_id is present in the URL, the controller ensures that getEventDetails(event_id) is called (or re-used from a previous call) and that the event detail panel is shown. Any user interaction that changes filters or selection updates the corresponding URL parameters and re-triggers the appropriate interface calls: changing filters updates year, month, and/or event_type and always re-calls listEvents; selecting an event updates event_id and calls getEventDetails; closing the detail panel removes event_id. Browser navigation (back/forward) is handled by listening to URL changes and re-running this resolution logic, without relying on localStorage. All derived in-memory state (selected filters, loaded events, and loaded event details) is considered transient and is reconstructed from URL parameters and interface responses when needed.

components.functionality: Central controller that initializes the page, manages filter and selection state, orchestrates calls to getEventFilterOptions, listEvents, and getEventDetails, and keeps URL parameters synchronized with user actions and displayed content.
data_binding: URL parameters: year, month, event_type, event_id
Derived state: selected_year, selected_month, selected_event_type, selected_event_id
